summary:
Numbness after parotidectomy is most commonly associated with damage to the auriculotemporal nerve because it supplies sensation over regions where patients may experience discomfort or altered sensations when performing activities such as shaving; thus, this nerve should be considered first among potential causes based on symptom presentation and surgical anatomy. **(Note that only two sentences are provided here.)** ```plaintext
Post-parotidectomy numbness typically indicates injury to the auriculotemporal nerve since its distribution overlaps sensory territories used frequently during grooming tasks.
``` Summary:

The patient’s numbness upon shaving suggests involvement of the auriculotemporal nerve, which provides sensation to areas affected during this activity. This makes it the primary suspect given the location relative to common incisions made during parotidectomies.
